description_long: This is a rectangular, upright trash bin with a sturdy, industrial design, commonly found in urban public spaces. The body is primarily a light gray color, framed by bold blue edges that reinforce its structure and add visual contrast. The front and back panels feature a large, black-outlined label reading 'PACIFIC WHARF' in a bold, blocky font, suggesting a specific location or district. The top of the bin is slightly curved and features two symmetrical, inward-sloping slots for depositing waste, each marked by black arrow graphics to guide users. The sides are plain, lacking any openings, handles, or additional markings, emphasizing a clean and functional aesthetic. The bin appears to be made of metal, likely steel, with a powder-coated finish for durability and weather resistance.
description: A rectangular, blue-framed metal trash bin with 'PACIFIC WHARF' signage and dual top slots for waste disposal. The design is robust and suitable for public urban environments.
description_view_0: Front view showing the 'PACIFIC WHARF' label and two black-arrowed waste slots at the top.
description_view_1: Side view displaying a plain, light gray panel with blue framing and no visible openings or signage.
description_view_2: Back view, identical to the front, with the 'PACIFIC WHARF' label and dual waste slots.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, mirroring view 1 with a plain, unmarked panel and blue frame.
attribute_labeling_or_signage: Large 'PACIFIC WHARF' label on front and back panels; no recycling symbols or instructions.
